summary: Session 4: live dogfood against the real GitHub Copilot CLI (v1.0.68) and operator UAT. Catalog discovery on the operator's seat (--seat-id op-personal) confirmed 15/18 models across anthropic/openai/google, matching S1 evidence. Live dogfood surfaced and fixed THREE real defects the S2/S3 fake-spawner suite never exercised because it modeled the wrong wire shape: (1) get_cli_version() stored the real CLI's multi-line --version banner verbatim as cli_version, producing a lockfile the module's own loader could not parse back (reproduced: ValueError, an unescaped literal newline inside a quoted TOML string) -- fixed to keep only the first line. (2) SEVERE: _success_result() read content/model/outputTokens directly off the assistant.message envelope, but the real CLI nests all message-type payload fields under a "data" key -- every real dispatch silently returned content="" as a false success -- fixed by unwrapping final_message.get("data", {}) before field extraction. (3) SEVERE, compounding: default_spawner used Popen(text=True) with no explicit encoding, so on this Windows seat stdout/stderr decoded as cp1252; the real CLI's UTF-8 JSONL routinely contains bytes cp1252 cannot decode (e.g. an em dash in ordinary model prose), which crashed the reader thread mid-stream, left the child process blocked on a full OS pipe, and caused the caller's own 300s total-timeout to force-kill it -- misclassifying a local decode bug as "total-timeout" and masking the real cause (reproduced live: a verify() call hung ~300s under the old code; the identical prompt via Popen with encoding="utf-8" completed in ~7s) -- fixed with encoding="utf-8", errors="replace" explicit. All three covered by new/updated regression tests. A routed code-review pass (auto-verified by its own gemini-pro pass) found 6 further findings: 2 fixed (a utf8_replacement_seen observability flag added to transport_metadata; a trailing-newline nit), 3 adjudicated-no-fix with recorded reasoning (result-event field guards -- no live consumer, one claim about a nonexistent exitCode read; a drift-detection false-positive concern -- unreachable because the old bug made lockfiles fail to LOAD, not silently mismatch; an absent-data-key design question -- matches the module's own existing absent-vs-malformed convention), 1 discarded as fabricated (a verifier cited a _read_and_process_stdout method and self._session_id attribute that do not exist anywhere in the file, confirmed by grep). Cross-provider session verification (routed, gemini-pro): VERIFIED, explicitly checking and confirming all three adjudication calls rather than re-flagging them blind. Re-ran the live dogfood after all fixes: route() generated via claude-sonnet-4.6 (anthropic), verify() cross-provider-verified via gpt-5.4 (openai), verdict VERIFIED, zero issues -- saved as s4-dogfood-evidence.json. Authored the ad-hoc per-set UAT checklist (7 walks: catalog discovery, one real routed call, one cross-provider verification, drift refusal induced, unavailable-model probe failure induced [safe stand-in for a genuine auth-failure walk, which S1 ruled out reproducing on the real working seat], invocation-breaker trip induced, api-profile regression confirmation) -- every walk was pre-run for real by the orchestrator before being handed to the operator, per the UAT-for-a-stranger bar set after the Set 077 UAT suspension. Operator walked all 7 items on their own seat and attested all 7 PASS; a saved-file anomaly (7 real filled-in rows plus 7 duplicate blank stub rows, apparently a tool/editor artifact) was flagged to the operator directly rather than silently resolved, then cleaned up per the operator's confirmation. router-config.yaml (transport.profile, max_invocations_per_session) was toggled several times during dogfood/UAT pre-verification and confirmed fully reverted to shipping defaults each time (final diff: none). Final suite: 2482 passed, 5 skipped, one pre-existing unrelated drift_guard failure (Set 077 and Set 078 both simultaneously in-progress by explicit operator direction) throughout. No version bump/release yet (Session 5's scope).

reason.specifics: Routed analysis (task_type=analysis, gemini-pro) recommended the incumbent continue into Session 5, citing strong context retention demonstrated diagnosing Session 4's live-CLI bugs, and suggested effort could step down from high to medium since Session 5's remaining scope (doc updates, release-ordering check, version bump, tag push) is procedurally simpler than Session 4's live debugging. The routed response also proposed a literal two-engine 'lead + second' team structure to satisfy the path-aware critique's >=2-provider requirement -- this misreads the architecture: the path-aware critique is already a separate, always-multi-provider stage (ai_router/prompt-templates/path-aware-critique.md) independent of which single engine orchestrates the session, exactly as every prior session in this set has done; the next_orchestrator field is a single-engine slot, not a team roster. Recorded the effort-level and continue-incumbent signal as recommended; the invented dual-engine-team framing is not adopted.
